summary: The narrowest workable BM-020 slice is a mirrored benchmark family with local repo fixtures, solution snapshots, summary plumbing, and frozen bundle manifests. Keep it benchmark-scoped; no compiler changes were needed.
ergonomics: The existing benchmark task pattern made it straightforward to add a mirrored pair, verifier script, prep checks, and summary assertions without touching unrelated compiler code.
The Clasp compiled schema contract shape is slightly misleading at first glance: projection metadata is under `__claspSchemas[name].schema`, not on the top-level schema entry object.
`run-benchmark.mjs` centralizes aliases/comparisons well, so one new family mostly means one runner edit plus prep/summary regression updates.
follow_ups: Add a shared scaffold/helper for mirrored benchmark families so task.json, prompt, AGENTS, verifier, prep assertions, and summary fixtures do not require copy-heavy edits.
Consider exposing projection metadata directly on the public compiled schema entry if that is meant to be consumed by demos and benchmark harnesses.
If BM-020 evolves further, add a second scenario that exercises row-level authorization proofs in addition to the current read/write/field-disclosure proof surface.
warnings: `freeze` for any Clasp family on the default path still needs `--allow-bootstrap-recovery true`; otherwise bundle generation fails before writing the manifest.

When debugging Clasp benchmark demos, do not assume `classificationPolicy` and `projectionSource` are top-level on `__claspSchemas[name]`; using that path gives `null` and will look like a broken projection when the compiler output is actually fine.
`benchmarks/test-series-summary.sh` expectations are sensitive to the exact synthetic timing math used by the summarizer, so update the asserted deltas only after checking real summarize output.
residual_risks: The benchmark proves proof-gated reads/writes/disclosures through mirrored demo surfaces; it does not add a compiler-native first-class proof type yet.
`bash scripts/verify-all.sh` leaves benchmark result/workspace artifacts under `benchmarks/results` and `benchmarks/workspaces`, which is normal for this suite but adds local churn.
